msgs.text:
Turn 40 — orientation/compactness audit; the over-G isomorphism is now genuinely global I re-read the attached problem_statement.md, mega_prompt.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md only. The target is exactly the existence of a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓH,πH∘Φ=πS,for the two quotients of F=P⋊G, where P is the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1 and the relators rS,rH are the ones in the statement.The remaining audit point after Turn 39 is not another Magnus layer. It is whether the inverse-limit automorphism can be made with one fixed relator orientation and over the full infinite tame quotient G, rather than merely over finite-looking coefficient approximations.The answer is yes.

1. Fix the relator orientation once and for all

The abelianized computation in Turn 1 used the source rowwS=(σ+3E,−2),which corresponds to using rS−1, not switching signs arbitrarily at later finite levels. The target row iswH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E).

Turn 1 then built an idempotent-by-idempotent matrix U∈GL2 (Λ), Λ=Z2 [[G]], carrying wS to wH. On the (1−E)-piece this is the swap, and on the Ef-piece the matrix U0 has determinant s, a unit.

So define, globally and permanently,ρS:=rS−1.

The finite and profinite constructions should requireΦ(ρS)=rH.

This is enough, because rS and rS−1 have the same closed normal closure.2. The finite solution sets are genuinely finite, even though G is not finite Use the corrected two-parameter truncation from the history, not the false degree-only quotient. Let

M=Λ⟨⟨X0,X1⟩⟩,I=(X0,X1),and for a Magnus degree N and an open two-sided ideal J⊂Λ, setPN,J:=P∩(1+IN+JM).

Then P/PN,J is finite, andN,J⋂PN,J=1.

Define FN,J:=F/PN,J. This still has quotient G, but finite pro-2 kernel P/PN,J.

For each pair (N,J), define SN,J to be the set of over-G automorphismsΦN,J:FN,J→FN,J such thatΦN,J(ρS)=rH in FN,J, and such that the induced first-layer G-operator map is the fixed matrix UmodJ.This set is finite. An over-G automorphism is determined by finitely many values in the finite group P/PN,J: the images of the two G-operator generators and the cocycle values on the topological generators σ,τ of G, subject to the finite cocycle relation coming from τσ=τ2.

3. Nonemptiness of every SN,J The prior sweep now supplies the needed finite-level solvability.

First, the H1 -level obstruction vanishes via the matrix U∈GL2(Λ).

Second, after this H1 -change, all non-Ef idempotent pieces are unit-controlled. The only delicate piece is the Ef-local coefficient ring

R=EfΛ≃Z2 [[t]],t=s−1.

The residual Ef,mod2,s=1 problem was reduced toS(Y0,Y1,q)=X2,and Turn 18 proved the all-order residual inductionCm=Lm (m≥5),so every odd layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression.Third, the coefficient tower over R is now closed. The raw local row(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t)is exactly Nielsen-equivalent to the normalized row (2,t).The first t-layer is contractible, the first 2-Bockstein secondary tower satisfiesBd↠Hd(2)(d≥4),and Turn 39 repairs the higher coefficient proof using the normalized Koszul–Witt cascade. In that repaired form, every m=(2,t)-adic coefficient obstruction is either an ordinary Koszul lower ghost, an α-multiple of the already-cleared first 2-Bockstein class, or a later Magnus-degree Witt tail handled by triangular induction.Therefore, for every finite coefficient-and-degree truncation (N,J),SN,J=∅.4. Compactness gives a compatible global automorphism

If (M,K) refines (N,J), reduction gives a mapSM,K→SN,J.For any finite list of indices, choose a common refinement and a solution there; reducing gives a compatible point on the finite subdiagram. Since the SN,J are finite nonempty sets, compactness givesN,JlimSN,J=∅.

Choose a compatible family (ΦN,J). Since

F≃N,JlimFN,J,this family defines a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F.

For every N,J,Φ(ρS)rH−1∈PN,J.

Intersecting over all (N,J) givesΦ(ρS)=rH inside F.

Because ρS=rS−1,Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F.

Thus Φ descends to an isomorphism

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F∼

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,i.e.ΓS∼ΓH.Since every finite-stage map was over G, the limit is over G, so the induced quotient isomorphism satisfiesπH∘Φ=πS.

ThereforeΓS≅GΓH.This turn closes a small but important audit gap: the sign is fixed globally as ρS=rS−1, and the compactness argument works over the full G because only the pro-2 kernel is truncated; the cocycle data into each finite kernel still has only finitely many possibilities.

ESTABLISHED: With the fixed orientation ρS=rS−1, the finite coefficient-and-degree solution sets SN,J are finite, nonempty, and compatible. Compactness produces a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH, hence Φ identifies the closed normal relator subgroups and descends to ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains in the sweep, conditional on the finite rank/base computations from Turns 1–37 and the Koszul–Witt coefficient induction from Turn 39 being accepted as established. The remaining work is reproducibility: write the proof as a clean note and provide machine-checkable or hand-checkable certificates for the finite base ranks.

NEXT: Convert the sweep into a standalone proof with five named lemmas: H1 Nielsen equivalence, residual restricted-Lie induction, first 2-coefficient secondary surjectivity Bd↠Hd(2), normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction, and coefficient-and-degree compactness descent.[TURN40-ORIENTATION-COMPACTNESS-AUDIT-ISO]
